english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrāhīm, who said: If the master permits the marriage, divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ بِإِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ، فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Ḥuṣayn → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Ḥuṣayn, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: If he marries with his master's permission, divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ مُبَارَكِ بْنِ فَضَالَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ، وَحُذَيْفَةَ، فِي الْعَبْدِ يَتَزَوَّجُ بِإِذْنِ مَوَالِيهِ فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Al-Faḍl ibn Dukayn → Mubārak ibn Faḍālah → Ibrāhīm ibn Abī Ismā‘īl → ‘Alī and ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn ‘Awf and Ḥudhayfah
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Faḍl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Mubārak ibn Faḍālah, from Ibrāhīm ibn Abī Ismā‘īl, from ‘Alī, ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn ‘Awf, and Ḥudhayfah, regarding the slave who marries with the permission of his masters: Divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ قَالُوا: إِذَا كَانَتِ الْمَمْلُوكَةُ لِغَيْرِهِ، أَوْ كَانَتْ عِنْدَهُ، وَقَدْ أَذِنَ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَهَا فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْمَمْلُوكِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘tamir → Layth → ‘Aṭā’, Ṭāwūs, and Mujāhid
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘tamir narrated to us, from Layth, from ‘Aṭā’, Ṭāwūs, and Mujāhid, who said: If the slave woman belongs to someone else, or she is with him, and he has permitted him to marry her, then divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي: الرَّجُلُ يُنْكِحُ مَمْلُوكَهُ مَمْلُوكَتَهُ، هَلْ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْزِعَهَا مِنْهُ بِغَيْرِ طِيبِ نَفْسٍ مِنْهُ؟ قَالَ: بِئْسَ مَا صَنَعَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → His father
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, who said: I said to my father: A man marries his slave to his slave girl; is it right for him to take her away from him without his consent? He said: What he did is evil.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا نَكَحَ الْعَبْدُ بِإِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ، فَإِنَّ الطَّلَاقَ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ، إِنْ شَاءَ طَلَّقَ، وَإِنْ شَاءَ أَمْسَكَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, that he used to say: If the slave marries with his master's permission, divorce is in the hand of the slave; if he wishes, he divorces, and if he wishes, he retains.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ: إِنَّ جَابِرَ بْنَ زَيْدٍ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا زَوَّجَ السَّيِّدُ فَإِنَّ الطَّلَاقَ بِيَدِهِ فَقَالَ: كَذَبَ جَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, who said: I said to Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr: Jābir ibn Zayd used to say: If the master marries him off, divorce is in his hand. He said: Jābir ibn Zayd lied.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ: الطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ مَنْ يَمْلِكُ الْبُضْعَ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muḥammad, who said: Divorce is in the hand of the one who possesses the marriage bond (buḍ‘).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَاتِمُ بْنُ وَرْدَانَ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ قَالَ: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْعَبْدُ بِإِذْنِ مَوْلَاهُ فَطَلَاقُهُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ، لَيْسَ لِسَيِّدِهِ أَنْ يُطَلِّقَ عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥātim ibn Wardān → Burd → Makḥūl
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥātim ibn Wardān narrated to us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, who said: If the slave marries with the permission of his master, his divorce is in the hand of the slave; his master has no right to divorce on his behalf.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: إِذَا زَوَّجَ الرَّجُلُ عَبْدَهُ أَوْ أَذِنَ لَهُ فِي التَّزَوُّجِ، فَإِنَّ الطَّلَاقَ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhrī, who said: If a man marries off his slave or permits him to marry, then divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ قَالَ: مَنْ زَوَّجَ عَبْدَهُ أَمَةً بِمَهْرِ بِنْتِهِ، لَا يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْزِعَهَا مِنْهُ، وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ فَرْجُهَا حَتَّى يَمُوتَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → Juwaybir → Al-Ḍaḥḥāk
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Juwaybir, from Al-Ḍaḥḥāk, who said: Whoever marries his slave to a slave girl as the dowry of his daughter, it is not right for him to take her away from him, nor is her private part lawful for him until he dies.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبِيدَةُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: إِذَا أَذِنَ السَّيِّدُ لِعَبْدِهِ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ، فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abīdah ibn Ḥumayd → ‘Ubayd Allāh → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abīdah ibn Ḥumayd narrated to us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: If the master permits his slave to marry, divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَجَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: الطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-Wahhāb → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Anas ibn Mālik and Ibn ‘Abbās and Jābir ibn ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Anas ibn Mālik, Ibn ‘Abbās, and Jābir ibn ‘Abd Allāh, that they said: Divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ قَالَ: إِذَا زَوَّجْتَ عَبْدَكَ أَمَتَكَ، ثُمَّ بِعْتَهُ فَلَيْسَ لَكَ أَنْ تَمْنَعَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib, who said: If you marry your slave to your slave girl, then you sell him, you have no right to prevent him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ، قَالَا: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْعَبْدُ بِإِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib and Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib and Al-Ḥasan, who both said: If the slave marries with his master's permission, divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ قَالَ: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْعَبْدُ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ، فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ سَيِّدِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Ḥuṣayn → ‘Āmir
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Ḥuṣayn, from ‘Āmir, who said: If the slave marries without his master's permission, divorce is in the hand of his master.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، قَالَ: نا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْعَبْدِ يَتَزَوَّجُ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ قَالَ: إِنْ شَاءَ السَّيِّدُ أَبْطَلَ ذَلِكَ، وَإِنْ شَاءَ سَكَتَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Ādam → Isrā’īl → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn Ādam narrated to us, saying: Isrā’īl narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, regarding the slave who marries without his master's permission. He said: If the master wishes, he invalidates that, and if he wishes, he remains silent.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْعَبْدُ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِ سَيِّدِهِ فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ السَّيِّدِ، وَإِذَا تَزَوَّجَ بِإِذْنِهِ فَالطَّلَاقُ بِيَدِ الْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: If the slave marries without his master's permission, divorce is in the hand of the master. If he marries with his permission, divorce is in the hand of the slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا الْعُمَرِيُّ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Al-‘Umarī → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-‘Umarī narrated to us, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بَقِيُّ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ قَالَ: نَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَتِ النَّصْرَانِيَّةُ قَبْلَ زَوْجِهَا فَهِيَ أَمْلَكُ بِنَفْسِهَا

english.narrator: Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān Baqī ibn Makhlad → Abū Bakr ‘Abd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah → ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → Khālid → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān Baqī ibn Makhlad narrated to us, saying: Abū Bakr ‘Abd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah narrated to us, saying: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from Khālid, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: If a Christian woman accepts Islam before her husband, she has more right to herself.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ الْحَسَنَ، وَعُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ قَالَا: فِي النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ تُسْلِمُ تَحْتَ زَوْجِهَا، قَالَا: الْإِسْلَامُ أَخْرَجَهَا مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān → His father → Al-Ḥasan and ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from his father, that Al-Ḥasan and ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz said regarding a Christian woman who accepts Islam while married to her husband: Islam has removed her from him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ تُسْلِمُ تَحْتَ زَوْجِهَا، قَالَ: يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → Ḥajjāj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding a Christian woman who accepts Islam while married to her husband. He said: They are separated.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، فِي نَصْرَانِيٍّ تَكُونُ تَحْتَهُ نَصْرَانِيَّةٌ، فَتُسْلِمُ، قَالُوا: إِنْ أَسْلَمَ مَعَهَا فَهِيَ امْرَأَتُهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يُسْلِمْ فُرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Muḥammad al-Muḥāribī → Layth → ‘Aṭā’, Ṭāwūs, and Mujāhid
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Muḥammad al-Muḥāribī narrated to us, from Layth, from ‘Aṭā’, Ṭāwūs, and Mujāhid, regarding a Christian man married to a Christian woman, and she accepts Islam. They said: If he accepts Islam with her, she is his wife; if he does not accept Islam, they are separated.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنِ السَّفَّاحِ بْنِ مَطَرٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ كُرْدُوسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي تَغْلِبَ يُقَالُ لَهُ: عَبَّادُ بْنُ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ زُرْعَةَ، كَانَتْ عِنْدَهُ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْ بَنِي تَمِيمٍ، وَكَانَ عَبَّادٌ نَصْرَانِيًّا، فَأَسْلَمَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ، وَأَبَى أَنْ يُسْلِمَ، فَفَرَّقَ عُمَرُ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ali b. Mushir → Shaybani → Saffah b. Matar → Dawud b. Kurdus
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Ali ibn Mushir told us, from Al-Shaybani, from Al-Saffah ibn Matar, from Dawud ibn Kurdus, who said: There was a man from Banu Taghlib called 'Abbad ibn al-Nu'man ibn Zur'ah. He had a wife from Banu Tamim. 'Abbad was a Christian, so his wife accepted Islam, but he refused to accept Islam. 'Umar separated them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ قَبْلَ زَوْجِهَا، انْقَطَعَ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا مِنَ النِّكَاحِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: "If a woman accepts Islam before her husband, the marriage between them is severed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ عَلْقَمَةَ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ بَنِي تَغْلِبَ، يُقَالُ لَهُ: عَبَّادُ بْنُ النُّعْمَانِ، فَكَانَ تَحْتَهُ امْرَأَةٌ مِنْ بَنِي تَمِيمٍ فَأَسْلَمَتْ، فَدَعَاهُ عُمَرُ فَقَالَ: إِمَّا أَنْ تُسْلِمَ، وَإِمَّا أَنْ أَنْزِعَهَا مِنْكَ فَأَبَى أَنْ يُسْلِمَ، فَنَزَعَهَا مِنْهُ عُمَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad b. al-'Awwam → Al-Shaybani → Yazid b. 'Alqamah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam told us, from Al-Shaybani, from Yazid ibn 'Alqamah, that a man from Banu Taghlib called 'Abbad ibn al-Nu'man had a wife from Banu Tamim. She accepted Islam, so 'Umar called him and said: "Either you accept Islam, or I will take her away from you." He refused to accept Islam, so 'Umar took her away from him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِي الْيَهُودِيِّ وَالنَّصْرَانِيِّ تُسْلِمُ امْرَأَتُهُ عِنْدَهُ، يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. Fudayl → Mutarrif → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn Fudayl told us, from Mutarrif, from Al-Hakam, regarding the Jew and the Christian whose wife accepts Islam while with him: "They are separated."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ شُبْرُمَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَصْرَانِيٍّ وَامْرَأَتُهُ نَصْرَانِيَّةٌ فَأَسْلَمَتْ، قَالَ: فُرِّقَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu'bah → Ibn Shubrumah → 'Amr b. Murrah → Sa'id b. Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ghundar told us, from Shu'bah, from Ibn Shubrumah, from 'Amr ibn Murrah, who said: I asked Sa'id ibn Jubayr about a Christian man and his Christian wife, then she accepted Islam. He said: "They are separated."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ قَالَ: يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Salim → Sa'id
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Sufyan, from Salim, from Sa'id, who said: "They are separated."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَتِ النَّصْرَانِيَّةُ امْرَأَةُ الْيَهُودِيِّ، أَوِ النَّصْرَانِيِّ كَانَ أَحَقَّ بِبُضْعِهَا لِأَنَّ لَهُ عَهْدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. Fudayl → Mutarrif → 'Amir → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn Fudayl told us, from Mutarrif, from 'Amir, from 'Ali, who said: "If a Christian woman, the wife of a Jew or a Christian, accepts Islam, he has more right to her vulva (sexual intimacy) because he has a covenant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، وَشُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا مَا دَامَا فِي دَارِ الْهِجْرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hisham, Shu'bah → Qatadah → Sa'id b. al-Musayyib → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Hisham and Shu'bah, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, from 'Ali, who said: "He has more right to her as long as they are in the abode of emigration (Dar al-Hijrah)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الْخِطْمِيِّ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ، كَتَبَ يُخَيَّرْنَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Yazid → Ibn Sirin → 'Abd Allah b. Yazid al-Khatmi → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Yazid, from Ibn Sirin, from 'Abd Allah ibn Yazid al-Khatmi, that 'Umar wrote: "They should be given the choice."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا مَا كَانَتْ فِي الْمِصْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "He has more right to her as long as she is in the city."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: يُقَرَّانِ عَلَى نِكَاحِهِمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Sufyan, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "They are confirmed in their marriage."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّ هَانِئَ بْنَ قَبِيصَةَ الشَّيْبَانِيَّ وَكَانَ نَصْرَانِيًّا، كَانَ عِنْدَهُ أَرْبَعُ نِسْوَةٍ فَأَسْلَمْنَ، فَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ أَنْ يُقْرَرْنَ عِنْدَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, that Hani' ibn Qabisah al-Shaybani was a Christian and had four wives who accepted Islam. So 'Umar ibn al-Khattab wrote that they remain with him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّ نَصْرَانِيَّةً أَسْلَمَتْ تَحْتَ نَصْرَانِيٍّ، فَأَرَادُوا أَنْ يَنْزِعُوهَا مِنْهُ، فَرَحَلُوا إِلَى عُمَرَ فَخَيَّرَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, that a Christian woman accepted Islam while married to a Christian, and they wanted to take her away from him. So they traveled to 'Umar, and he gave her the choice.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ الْحَسَنَ، وَعُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ قَالَا: تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu'tamir b. Sulayman → Abu Mu'tamir → Al-Hasan & 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman told us, from his father, that Al-Hasan and 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz said: "It is an irrevocable divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ الرَّجُلُ وَامْرَأَتُهُ مُشْرِكَيْنِ، فَأَسْلَمَتْ وَأَبَى أَنْ يُسْلِمَ، بَانَتْ مِنْهُ بِوَاحِدَةٍ وَقَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ: مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abdah b. Sulayman → Sa'id → Qatadah → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abdah ibn Sulayman told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan, who said: "If a man and his wife are polytheists, and she accepts Islam but he refuses to accept Islam, she is separated from him with one divorce." And 'Ikrimah said the like.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: تَفْرِيقُ الْإِمَامِ تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ma'n b. 'Isa → Ibn Abi Dhi'b → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ma'n ibn 'Isa told us, from Ibn Abi Dhi'b, from Al-Zuhri, who said: "The separation by the Imam is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّ امْرَأَةَ عِكْرِمَةَ بْنِ أَبِي جَهْلٍ أَسْلَمَتْ قَبْلَهُ، ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَ وَهِيَ فِي الْعِدَّةِ، فَرُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِ، وَذَلِكَ عَلَى عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-Salam b. Harb → Ishaq b. 'Abd Allah b. Abi Farwah → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-Salam ibn Harb told us, from Ishaq ibn 'Abd Allah ibn Abi Farwah, from Al-Zuhri, that the wife of 'Ikrimah ibn Abi Jahl accepted Islam before him, then he accepted Islam while she was in her waiting period, so she was returned to him. And that was during the time of the Prophet ﷺ.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَ وَهِيَ فِي عِدَّتِهَا فَهِيَ امْرَأَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abdah b. Sulayman → Sa'id → Qatadah → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abdah ibn Sulayman told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Mujahid, who said: "If he accepts Islam while she is in her waiting period, she is his wife."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: إِنْ أَسْلَمَ وَهِيَ فِي الْعِدَّةِ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Ibn Abi Najih → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Ibn Abi Najih, from 'Ata', who said: "If he accepts Islam while she is in the waiting period, he has more right to her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ قَالَ: هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا مَا دَامَتْ فِي الْعِدَّةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ubayd Allah → Sufyan → 'Amr b. Maymun → 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Ubayd Allah told us, from Sufyan, from 'Amr ibn Maymun, from 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz, who said: "He has more right to her as long as she is in the waiting period."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَ وَهِيَ فِي الْعِدَّةِ فَهِيَ امْرَأَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad b. al-'Awwam → Hajjaj → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam told us, from Hajjaj, from 'Ata', who said: "If he accepts Islam while she is in the waiting period, she is his wife."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، قَالَ: نا عُمَرُ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ قَالَ: "إِذَا أَسْلَمَ الزَّوْجُ بَعْدَ امْرَأَتِهِ خَيَّرَهَا مَا دَامَتْ فِي الْعِدَّةِ، أَوْ قَالَ: هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا مَا دَامَتْ فِي الْعِدَّةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Yunus → 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Yunus, who said: 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz told us, saying: "If the husband accepts Islam after his wife, he gives her the choice as long as she is in the waiting period," or he said: "He has more right to her as long as she is in the waiting period."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: أَيُّمَا يَهُودِيٍّ أَوْ نَصْرَانِيٍّ أَسْلَمَ، ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ فَهُمَا عَلَى نِكَاحِهِمَا إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ فَرَّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا سُلْطَانٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu'tamir b. Sulayman → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman told us, from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, who said: "Any Jew or Christian who accepts Islam, then his wife accepts Islam, they remain in their marriage unless a ruler has separated them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: لَيْسَ فِي الظِّهَارِ وَقْتٌ، وَلَا يَدْخُلُ فِيهِ إِيلَاءٌ، وَإِنْ تَطَاوَلَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad b. al-'Awwam → Sa'id → Qatadah → Sa'id b. al-Musayyib & Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib and Al-Hasan, that they said: "There is no time limit in Zihar, and Ila' (oath of abstinence) does not enter into it, even if it lasts long."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad → Sa'id → Abu Ma'shar → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abbad told us, from Sa'id, from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُظَاهِرُ مِنِ امْرَأَتِهِ وَلَا يُوَقِّتُ أَجَلًا، قَالَ: لَا تَبِينُ مِنْهُ امْرَأَتُهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَقَعْ عَلَيْهَا مَا دَامَ يَتَلَوَّمُ فِي الْكَفَّارَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Jarir told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who performs Zihar from his wife and does not set a time limit. He said: "His wife is not separated from him, even if he does not have intercourse with her as long as he is delaying the expiation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الظِّهَارِ وَقْتٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "There is no time limit in Zihar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الظِّهَارِ وَقْتٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: "There is no time limit in Zihar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الظِّهَارِ وَقْتٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Layth, from Tawus, who said: "There is no time limit in Zihar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ قَرَبْتُهَا سَنَةً فَهِيَ عَلَيْهِ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّهِ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ: لَا يَدْخُلُ الْإِيلَاءُ فِي الظِّهَارِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi, regarding a man who said to his wife: "If I approach her for a year, she is to me like my mother's back." Al-Sha'bi said: "Ila' does not enter into Zihar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ قَالَا: "إِذَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: هِيَ عَلَيْهِ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ، فَمَضَتْ أَرْبَعَةُ أَشْهُرٍ فَهُوَ إِيلَاءٌ وَإِذَا قَالَ: هِيَ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي، فَتَرَكَهَا سَنَةً فَلَيْسَ إِيلَاءً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sahl b. Yusuf → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam & Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Sahl ibn Yusuf told us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam and Hammad, who said: "If a man says to his wife: 'She is to me like my mother's back for four months,' and four months pass, it is Ila'. And if he says: 'She is to me like my mother's back,' and leaves her for a year, it is not Ila'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: لَا يَدْخُلُ الْإِيلَاءُ فِي الظِّهَارِ، وَلَا ظِهَارٌ فِي الْإِيلَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs b. Ghiyath → Ibn Jurayj → Ibrahim → A man → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs ibn Ghiyath told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ibrahim, from a man, from 'Ali, who said: "Ila' does not enter into Zihar, nor Zihar into Ila'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: "لَيْسَ فِي الظِّهَارِ وَقْتٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يَقُولَ: إِنْ قَرَبْتُكِ، فَإِنْ قَالَ: فَتَرَكَهَا أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ بِالْإِيلَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "There is no time limit in Zihar unless he says: 'If I approach you.' If he says that and leaves her for four months, she is separated from him by Ila'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنِ ابْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: "إِذَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: أَنْتِ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي، إِنْ قَرَبْتُكِ، فَإِنْ قَرَبَهَا وَقَعَ الظِّهَارُ، وَإِنْ تَرَكَهَا أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ بِالْإِيلَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Ibn Salim → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar told us, from Ibn Salim, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "If a man says to his wife: 'You are to me like my mother's back if I approach you,' if he approaches her, Zihar occurs. And if he leaves her for four months, she is separated from him by Ila'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: هُوَ إِيلَاءٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya b. Sa'id → 'Amr → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, from 'Amr, from Al-Hasan, who said: "It is Ila'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ قَرَبْتُكِ فَأَنْتِ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي فَتَرَكَهَا أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ قَالَ: لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya b. Sa'id → Ibn Jurayj → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', regarding a man who said to his wife: "If I approach you, you are to me like my mother's back," and he left her for four months. He said: "It is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَعَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَا: "إِذَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ قَرَبْتُكِ فَأَنْتِ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي، فَإِنْ قَرَبَهَا فِي أَرْبَعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ فَهُوَ ظِهَارٌ، وَقَدْ وَقَّتَ عَلَيْهِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْرَبْهَا حَتَّى تَمْضِيَ أَرْبَعَةُ أَشْهُرٍ فَهُوَ إِيلَاءٌ، وَقَدْ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ بِوَاحِدَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-Rahim b. Sulayman → Sa'id → Qatadah → Al-Hasan & Abu Ma'shar → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan; and from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, who said: "If a man says to his wife: 'If I approach you, you are to me like my mother's back,' if he approaches her within four months, it is Zihar, and he has set a time for it. And if he does not approach her until four months pass, it is Ila', and she is separated from him by one [divorce]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا شَبَابَةُ، قَالَ نا شُعْبَةُ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ قَالَا: سَأَلْتُهُمَا عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ قَرَبْتُكِ سَنَةً فَأَنْتِ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي قَالَا: إِذَا مَضَتْ أَرْبَعَةُ أَشْهُرٍ فَهِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ وَبِهِ يَأْخُذُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Shababah → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam & Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Shababah told us, he said: Shu'bah told us, from Al-Hakam and Hammad, who said: I asked them about a man who said to his wife: "If I approach you for a year, you are to me like my mother's back." They said: "If four months pass, it is a divorce." And Abu Bakr follows this.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: الْخُلْعُ تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنٌ، وَالْإِيلَاءُ، وَالْمُبَارَأَةُ كَذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu al-Ahwas told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "Khul' is an irrevocable divorce, and Ila' and Mubara'ah are likewise."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عَطَاءٌ: كُلُّ طَلَاقٍ كَانَ نِكَاحُهُ مُسْتَقِيمًا إِذَا تَفَرَّقَا فِي ذَلِكَ النِّكَاحِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَتَكَلَّمْ بِالطَّلَاقِ فَهِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ، الْمُبَارَأَةُ وَاحِدَةٌ بِالْفِدَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Ibn Jurayj → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: 'Ata' said: "Every divorce whose marriage was proper, if they separate in that marriage, even if he does not utter divorce, it is one [divorce]. Mubara'ah is one [divorce] by ransom."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ الزُّهْرِيَّ كَانَ يَقُولُ: الْمُبَارَأَةُ أَشَدُّ الطَّلَاقِ قَالَ: مَا نَرَاهُ إِذَا أَخَذَ مِنْهَا شَيْئًا افْتَدَتْ بِهِ إِلَّا بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْخُلْعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Kathir b. Hisham → Ja'far → 'Abd al-Karim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Kathir ibn Hisham told us, from Ja'far, who said: I said to 'Abd al-Karim: "It reached me that Al-Zuhri used to say: 'Mubara'ah is the severest divorce.'" He said: "We do not see it, if he took something from her by which she ransomed herself, except as the status of Khul'."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ قَالَا: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad b. al-'Awwam → 'Amr b. 'Amir → Qatadah → Sa'id b. al-Musayyib & Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam told us, from 'Amr ibn 'Amir, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib and Al-Hasan, who said: "Every separation is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ كَانَ مِنْ قِبَلِ الرَّجُلِ فَهِيَ طَلَاقٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-Salam b. Harb → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-Salam ibn Harb told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "Every separation that comes from the man is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَالَ: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ فَهِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. Yazid → Abu al-A'la → Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn Yazid told us, from Abu al-A'la, from Qatadah, who said: "Every separation is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ فَهِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd b. Hubab → Sufyan → Ibn Jurayj → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Zayd ibn Hubab told us, from Sufyan, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', who said: "Every separation is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ فَهِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sa'id → Abu Ma'shar → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Sa'id, from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, who said: "Every separation is an irrevocable divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: كُلُّ فُرْقَةٍ طَلَاقٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd b. al-Hubab → Sufyan → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Zayd ibn al-Hubab told us, from Sufyan, from Layth, from Tawus, who said: "Every separation is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَعَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، وَعَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ قَالُوا: إِذَا كَانَتِ الْأَمَةُ تَحْتَ الْحُرِّ، فَأُعْتِقَتْ، فَاخْتَارَتْ فَكَانَ فُرْقَةً بِغَيْرِ طَلَاقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Mughirah, Layth, Muhammad b. Salim → Ibrahim, Tawus, 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Sharik told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim; and from Layth, from Tawus; and from Muhammad ibn Salim, from 'Amir, who said: "If a slave girl is married to a free man, and she is freed and chooses herself, it is a separation without divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: إِذَا أُعْتِقَتْ فَخُيِّرَتْ، فَاخْتَارَتْ نَفْسَهَا، فَهِيَ فُرْقَةٌ بِغَيْرِ طَلَاقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If she is freed and given the choice, and she chooses herself, it is a separation without divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: هِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: "It is a divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ: إِذَا اخْتَارَتْ نَفْسَهَا فَهِيَ تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Hisham → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-A'la told us, from Hisham, from Muhammad, who said: "If she chooses herself, it is an irrevocable divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ شِئْتِ فَأَنْتِ طَالِقٌ قَالَ: إِنْ شَاءَتْ فَهِيَ طَالِقٌ، وَإِنْ لَمْ تَشَأْ فَلَا شَيْءَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr b. 'Ayyash → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who says to his wife: "If you wish, you are divorced." He said: "If she wishes, she is divorced; and if she does not wish, it is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، "فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: أَنْتِ طَالِقٌ إِنْ شِئْتِ، فَقَالَتْ: فَقَدْ شِئْتُ فَقَالَ: هِيَ طَالِقٌ، وَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِالرَّجْعَةِ، وَإِذَا قَالَ: إِنْ شِئْتُ طَلَّقْتُكِ، فَقَالَتْ: قَدْ شِئْتُ، قَالَ: إِنْ شَاءَ لَمْ يُطَلِّقْهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hammad b. Zayd → Hafs b. Sulayman → Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Hammad ibn Zayd, from Hafs ibn Sulayman, from Hasan, regarding a man who said to his wife: "You are divorced if you wish," and she said: "I wish." He said: "She is divorced, and he has more right to take her back. And if he said: 'If I wish, I will divorce you,' and she said: 'I wish,' he said: If he wishes, he does not divorce her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، مَوْلَى آلِ سَمُرَةَ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ بْنِ قَائِدٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِنْ فَعَلْتِ كَذَا وَكَذَا، فَلَسْتِ لِي بِامْرَأَةٍ، فَفَعَلَتْ، فَانْطَلَقَتْ مَعَهُ إِلَى عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، فَقَالَ: مَا نَوَى، وَأَتَتْ مَعَهُ أَبَا عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ الْهُذَلِيَّ فَقَالَ: مَا نَوَى، وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ: لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Ibrahim b. Maymun → 'Urwah b. Qa'id
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Ibrahim ibn Maymun, the client of the family of Samurah, from 'Urwah ibn Qa'id, that a man said to his wife: "If you do such-and-such, you are not my wife." She did it, so she went with him to 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla, who said: "[It is] whatever he intended." She also went with him to Abu 'Ubayd Allah al-Hudhali, who said: "[It is] whatever he intended." Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: "It is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ إِنَّ الْحَجَّاجَ يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: لَسْتِ لِي بِامْرَأَةٍ "فَقَالَ: تَطْلِيقَةٌ فَقَالَ سَعِيدٌ: مَا أَبْعَدَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Hisham al-Dastuwai → Qatadah → Sa'id b. al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ghundar told us, from Hisham al-Dastuwai, from Qatadah, who said: I said to Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib: "Al-Hajjaj narrates from his father that he said regarding a man who said to his wife: 'You are not my wife,' he said: '[It is] a divorce.'" Sa'id said: "How far [from correct]!"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، سُئِلَ جَابِرٌ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: مَا أَنْتِ لِي بِامْرَأَةٍ مِرَارًا وَهُوَ غَضْبَانُ، قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: مَا أَرَاهُ بَلَغَ هَذَا الْحَدَّ، إِلَّا وَهُوَ يُرِيدُ الطَّلَاقَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Jarir told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim: Jabir was asked about a man who said to his wife: "You are not my wife," repeatedly while angry. Ibrahim said: "I do not think he reached this extent unless he intends divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: لَسْتِ لِي بِامْرَأَةٍ قَالَ: مَا نَوَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-A'la told us, from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, that he said regarding a man who said to his wife: "You are not my wife." He said: "[It is] whatever he intended."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَوَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مَطَرٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَعَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: لَسْتُ لِي بِامْرَأَةٍ قَالَ: كِذْبَةٌ لَيْسَتْ بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. Sawa' → Sa'id → Matar → Al-Hasan & 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn Sawa' told us, from Sa'id, from Matar, from Al-Hasan and from 'Ata', regarding a man who said to his wife: "You are not my wife." He said: "It is a lie; it is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَوَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَالَ: إِذَا وَاجَهَهَا بِطَلَاقٍ، وَأَرَادَ الطَّلَاقَ فَهِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. Sawa' → Sa'id → Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn Sawa' told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, who said: "If he confronts her with divorce and intends divorce, it is one [divorce]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قِيلَ لَهُ: أَلَكَ امْرَأَةٌ؟ وَلَهُ امْرَأَةٌ فَقَالَ: لَا، فَقَالَ: كِذْبَةٌ كَذَبَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu'awiyah → Al-A'mash
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us, from Al-A'mash, that he was asked about a man who was asked: "Do you have a wife?" while he had a wife, and he said: "No." He said: "It is a lie he told."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: عَنْ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "كِذْبَةٌ، فِي الرَّجُلِ لَهُ امْرَأَةٌ فَسُئِلَ، أَلَكَ امْرَأَةٌ؟ فَيَقُولُ: لَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Sa'id → Qatadah → Al-Hasan → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan, from 'Umar, that he said: "It is a lie," regarding a man who has a wife and is asked: "Do you have a wife?" and he says: "No."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ghundar told us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, who said: "It is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: هُوَ كَاذِبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Ash'ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ghundar told us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, who said: "He is a liar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي رَجُلٍ يُقَالُ لَهُ: طَلَّقْتَ امْرَأَتَكَ؟ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ فَعَلَ، فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، فَقَالَ: يَقَعُ عَلَيْهَا الطَّلَاقُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu al-Ahwas told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who is asked: "Have you divorced your wife?" and he had not done so, but said: "Yes." He said: "Divorce befalls her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَبِي حُرَّةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ قِيلَ لَهُ: طَلَّقْتَ امْرَأَتَكَ؟ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ طَلَّقَهَا فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، فَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: فَقَدْ طُلِّقَتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Dawud → Abu Hurrah → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Dawud told us, from Abu Hurrah, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who was asked: "Have you divorced your wife?" and he had not divorced her, but said: "Yes." Al-Hasan said: "She is divorced."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُقَالُ لَهُ: طَلَّقْتَ؟ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ طَلَّقَ، فَيَقُولُ: نَعَمْ فَقَالَ: كِذْبَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Israil → Jabir → Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Isra'il, from Jabir, from 'Amir, regarding a man who is asked: "Have you divorced?" and he had not divorced, but says: "Yes." He said: "[It is] a lie."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هِشَامٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ شُبْرُمَةُ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: النِّيَّةُ فِيمَا خَفِيَ، فَأَمَّا فِيمَا ظَهَرَ فَلَا نِيَّةَ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hisham → Ibn Shubrumah → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hisham told us, from Ibn Shubrumah, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "Intention applies to what is hidden, but as for what is apparent, there is no intention in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَتِيكٍ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ وَاحِدَةً يَنْوِي ثَلَاثًا قَالَ: هِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad b. 'Atik → Khalid b. Dinar → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Muhammad ibn 'Atik told us, from Khalid ibn Dinar, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who divorced his wife once intending three. He said: "It is one."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ الْأَحْمَرِ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: هِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hamid b. Abd al-Rahman → Ja'far al-Ahmar → Mutarrif → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hamid ibn 'Abd al-Rahman told us, from Ja'far al-Ahmar, from Mutarrif, from Al-Hakam, who said: "It is one."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: هِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Layth → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs told us, from Layth, from Al-Hakam, who said: "It is one."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: أَنْتِ طَالِقٌ، وَأَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ ثَلَاثًا، قَالَ: فَسَأَلُوهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ؟ فَقَالَ: هِيَ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Layth → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs told us, from Layth, from Al-Hakam, regarding a man who said to his wife: "You are divorced," and gestured with his hand three times. He said: They asked him about that, and he said: "It is one."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ بَيَانٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ الشَّعْبِيُّ عَنْ أَبْوَابِ الطَّلَاقِ، فَقَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ: "سُئِلَ رَجُلٌ مَرَّةً: أَطَلَّقْتَ امْرَأَتَكَ؟ قَالَ: فَأَوْمَأَ بِيَدِهِ بِأَرْبَعِ أَصَابِعَ، وَلَمْ يَتَكَلَّمْ فَفَارَقَ امْرَأَتَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Bayan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Jarir told us, from Bayan, who said: Al-Sha'bi was asked about the chapters of divorce. Al-Sha'bi said: "A man was once asked: 'Did you divorce your wife?' He said: So he gestured with his hand with four fingers and did not speak, so he separated from his wife."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ قَالَ: اللِّعَانُ تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mubarak → Ma'mar → Qatadah → Sa'id b. al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn Mubarak told us, from Ma'mar, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: "Li'an is an irrevocable divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: اللِّعَانُ تَطْلِيقَةٌ بَائِنَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → Abu Hanifah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn Numayr told us, from Abu Hanifah, from Ibrahim, who said: "Li'an is an irrevocable divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: الْمُلَاعِنُ أَشَدُّ مِنَ الَّذِي يُطَلِّقُ ثَلَاثًا؟ فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hisham → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hisham told us, from Mughirah, who said: I said to Ibrahim: "Is the one who performs Li'an worse than the one who divorces three times?" He said: "Yes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: الْمُلَاعَنَةُ أَشَدُّ مِنَ الرَّجْمِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hushaym told us, from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "Mutual cursing (Mula'anah) is more severe than stoning."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، وَسُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، وَحُمَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ: سَمِعْنَا أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ: سَأَلْتُ عُمَرَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْبَحْرَيْنِ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ تَطْلِيقَةً أَوْ تَطْلِيقَتَيْنِ، فَتَزَوَّجَتْ، ثُمَّ إِنَّ زَوْجَهَا طَلَّقَهَا، ثُمَّ إِنَّ الْأَوَّلَ تَزَوَّجَهَا، عَلَى كَمْ هِيَ عِنْدَهُ؟ قَالَ: هِيَ عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sufyan b. 'Uyaynah → Al-Zuhri → 'Ubayd Allah, Sulayman b. Yasar & Humayd b. 'Abd al-Rahman → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah told us, from Al-Zuhri, from 'Ubayd Allah, Sulayman ibn Yasar, and Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman: We heard Abu Hurayrah saying: I asked 'Umar about a man from the people of Bahrain who divorced his wife once or twice, so she married [another], then her husband divorced her, then the first one married her [again]. On how many [divorces] is she with him? He said: "She is on what remains of the divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، وَحَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي لَيْلَى قَالَ: تَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِ بِمَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Ash'ath & Hajjaj → Al-Hakam → 'Abd al-Rahman b. Abi Layla → Abu Layla
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs told us, from Ash'ath and Hajjaj, from Al-Hakam, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla, from Abu Layla, who said: "She returns to him with what remains of the divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّ زِيَادًا، سَأَلَ عِمْرَانَ بْنَ حُصَيْنٍ، وَشُرَيْحًا، عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُطَلِّقُ امْرَأَتَهُ تَطْلِيقَةً أَوْ تَطْلِيقَتَيْنِ فَتَبِينُ، فَيَتَزَوَّجُهَا رَجُلٌ، فَيُطَلِّقُهَا أَوْ يَمُوتُ عَنْهَا، فَيَتَزَوَّجُهَا الْأَوَّلُ، عَلَى كَمْ تَكُونُ عِنْدَهُ؟ فَقَالَ عِمْرَانُ: عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ وَقَالَ شُرَيْحٌ: نِكَاحٌ جَدِيدٌ وَطَلَاقٌ جَدِيدٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi → Ziyad
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi, that Ziyad asked 'Imran ibn Husayn and Shurayh about a man who divorces his wife once or twice, and she becomes separated, then a man marries her and divorces her or dies leaving her, then the first one marries her. On how many [divorces] is she with him? 'Imran said: "On what remains of the divorce." And Shurayh said: "A new marriage and a new divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ، وَأَبيِ، وَأَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ، وَمُعَاذٌ يَقُولُونَ: تَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِ عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs b. Ghiyath → Hajjaj → 'Amr b. Shu'ayb
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs ibn Ghiyath told us, from Hajjaj, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, who said: 'Umar, Ubayy, Abu al-Darda', and Mu'adh used to say: "She returns to him on what remains."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ مَزِيدَةَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: لَا يَهْدِمُ الزَّوَاجَ إِلَّا الثَّلَاثُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' & Ali b. Hashim → Ibn Abi Layla → Mazidah b. Jabir → His Father → Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' and 'Ali ibn Hashim told us, from Ibn Abi Layla, from Mazidah ibn Jabir, from his father, from 'Ali, who said: "Nothing demolishes the marriage except three [divorces]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مَزِيدَةَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam → Mazidah b. Jabir → His Father → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ghundar told us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, from Mazidah ibn Jabir, from his father, from 'Ali, who said: "On what remains."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، قَالَ: قَضَى عُمَرُ، وَمُعَاذٌ، وَزَيْدٌ، وَأُبَيٌّ، وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ أَنَّهَا عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Hajjaj → 'Amr b. Shu'ayb
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar told us, from Hajjaj, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, who said: 'Umar, Mu'adh, Zayd, Ubayy, and 'Abd Allah ibn 'Umar judged that she is on what remains of the divorce.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ قَالَ: عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Yahya b. Sa'id → Sa'id → Abu Hurayrah → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar told us, from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from Sa'id, from Abu Hurayrah, from 'Umar, who said: "On what remains of the divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، نا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: هِيَ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى طَلَاقٍ مُسْتَقْبَلٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi → Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, Isma'il told us, from Al-Sha'bi, from Ibn 'Umar, who said: "She is with him on a new divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، وَسُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَا: هِيَ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى طَلَاقٍ جَدِيدٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Shu'bah & Sufyan → Hammad → Sa'id b. Jubayr → Ibn Abbas & Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Shu'bah and Sufyan, from Hammad, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas and Ibn 'Umar, who said: "She is with him on a new divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: هِيَ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى ثَلَاثٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, who said: "She is with him on three [divorces]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: "كَانَ أَصْحَابُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَقُولُونَ: يَهْدِمُ الثَّلَاثَ، وَلَا يَهْدِمُ الْوَاحِدَةَ وَالثِّنْتَيْنِ، يَعْنِي طَلَاقًا وَاحِدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Al-A'mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Waki' told us, from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, who said: The companions of 'Abd Allah used to say: "It destroys the three, but it does not destroy the one and the two," meaning one divorce.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: "كَانَ أَصْحَابُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَقُولُونَ: يَهْدِمُ الثَّلَاثَ، وَلَا يَهْدِمُ الْوَاحِدَةَ وَالثِّنْتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu'awiyah → Al-A'mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us, from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, who said: "The companions of 'Abd Allah used to say: 'It destroys the three, but it does not destroy the one and the two.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، "أَنَّ أَصْحَابَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: يَهْدِمُ الْوَاحِدَةَ وَالثِّنْتَيْنِ كَمَا يَهْدِمُ الثَّلَاثَ إِلَّا عُبَيْدَةَ، فَإِنَّهُ قَالَ: هِيَ كَمَا بَقِيَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Hajjaj → Talhah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Hafs told us, from Hajjaj, from Talhah, from Ibrahim, that the companions of 'Abd Allah used to say: "It destroys the one and the two just as it destroys the three, except 'Ubaydah, for he said: 'She is on what remains.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ قَالَ: عَلَى طَلَاقٍ جَدِيدٍ، وَعَلَى نِكَاحٍ جَدِيدٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi → Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi, from Shurayh, who said: "On a new divorce and on a new marriage."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ مَيْمُونٍ قَالَ: هِيَ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى طَلَاقٍ جَدِيدٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Kathir b. Hisham → Ja'far → Maymun
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Kathir ibn Hisham told us, from Ja'far, from Maymun, who said: "She is with him on a new divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ رَجَاءٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ قُلْتُ: رَجُلٌ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ تَطْلِيقَتَيْنِ فَبَانَتْ مِنْهُ، فَحَلَّتْ، فَتَزَوَّجَتْ زَوْجًا، فَدَخَلَ بِهَا، ثُمَّ مَاتَ عَنْهَا، أَوْ طَلَّقَهَا، فَرَجَعَتْ إِلَى الْأَوَّلِ عَلَى كَمْ هِيَ عِنْدَهُ؟ قَالَ: عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الطَّلَاقِ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: "فَطَلَّقَهَا أُخْرَى، فَبَانَتْ مِنْهُ، فَتَزَوَّجَتْ زَوْجًا فَدَخَلَ بِهَا، ثُمَّ مَاتَ عَنْهَا، أَوْ طَلَّقَهَا، فَرَجَعَتْ إِلَى زَوْجِهَا الْأَوَّلِ عَلَى كَمْ هِيَ عِنْدَهُ؟ قَالَ: هِيَ عَلَى مَا بَقِيَ قُلْتُ: فَطَلَّقَهَا أُخْرَى، فَحَلَّتْ فَتَزَوَّجَتْ زَوْجًا، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ بِهَا، ثُمَّ مَاتَ عَنْهَا، فَرَجَعَتْ إِلَى زَوْجِهَا الْأَوَّلِ، عَلَى كَمْ هِيَ عِنْدَهُ؟ قَالَ: هِيَ عَلَى ثَلَاثٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazid b. Harun → Ibn 'Awn → Raja' → Qabisah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Yazid ibn Harun told us, from Ibn 'Awn, from Raja', from Qabisah, who said: I said: "A man divorced his wife twice, and she became separated from him, then became lawful, so she married a husband who consummated the marriage with her, then he died or divorced her, so she returned to the first one. On how many is she with him?" He said: "On what remains of the divorce." He said: I said: "So he divorced her again, and she became separated from him, then she married a husband who consummated the marriage with her, then he died or divorced her, so she returned to her first husband. On how many is she with him?" He said: "She is on what remains." I said: "So he divorced her again, then she became lawful and married a husband, then he consummated the marriage with her, then he died, so she returned to her first husband. On how many is she with him?" He said: "She is on three."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: إِنْ دَخَلَ بِهَا فَإِنَّهَا عِنْدَهُ عَلَى ثَلَاثِ تَطْلِيقَاتٍ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ بِهَا فَإِنَّهَا عِنْدَهُ عَلَى بَقِيَّةِ الطَّلَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Jarir told us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If he has consummated the marriage with her, she is with him on three divorces. And if he has not consummated the marriage with her, she is with him on the remainder of the divorce."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَالَ: يَقَعُ عَلَيْهَا عِنْدَ كُلِّ طُهْرٍ مَرَّةً، ثُمَّ يُمْسِكُ حَتَّى تَطْهُرَ، فَإِذَا اسْتَبَانَ حَمْلُهَا بَانَتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Sa'id → Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-A'la told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, who said: "It occurs upon her at every purification once, then he withholds until she becomes pure. If her pregnancy becomes evident, she is separated."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: إِذَا حَمَلْتِ فَأَنْتِ طَالِقٌ، قَالَ: يَغْشَاهَا فَإِذَا تَطَهَّرَتْ مِنَ الْحَيْضِ، ثُمَّ يُمْسِكُ عَنْهَا إِلَى مِثْلِ ذَلِكَ وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ: يَغْشَاهَا حَتَّى تَحْمِلَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu'adh b. Mu'adh → Ash'ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Mu'adh ibn Mu'adh told us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, that he said regarding a man who said to his wife: "If you become pregnant, you are divorced." He said: "He has intercourse with her, then when she becomes pure from menstruation, he withholds from her until the like of that." And Ibn Sirin said: "He has intercourse with her until she becomes pregnant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَكِتَابِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: إِذَا سَبَقَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ بِالْإِسْلَامِ، فَلَا سَبِيلَ لَهُ عَلَيْهَا إِلَّا بِخِطْبَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Sa'id → Qatadah → Al-Hasan & 'Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: 'Abd al-A'la told us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan, 'Ikrimah, and the letter of 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz, that they said: "If one of them precedes the other in [accepting] Islam, he has no way to her except through a proposal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الْمَجُوسِيَّيْنِ، إِذَا أَسْلَمَا فَهُمَا عَلَى نِكَاحِهِمَا، وَإِنْ أَسْلَمَ أَحَدُهُمَا قَبْلَ صَاحِبِهِ انْقَطَعَ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا مِنَ النِّكَاحِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, regarding two Magians: "If they both accept Islam, they remain in their marriage. And if one of them accepts Islam before the other, the marriage between them is severed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، وَعَنِ الْحَسَنِ، مِثْلَهُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ: بَانَتْ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr b. 'Ayyash → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash told us, from Hisham, and from Al-Hasan, similar to it, except that he said: "She is separated from him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ وَالْمَرْأَةِ يَكُونَانِ مُشْرِكَيْنِ فَيُسْلِمَانِ، قَالَ: يَثْبُتُ نِكَاحُهُمَا، فَإِنْ أَسْلَمَ أَحَدُهُمَا قَبْلَ الْآخَرِ انْقَطَعَ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ الْمَجُوسَ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ غَيْرَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → 'Abd al-Malik → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr told us, he said: Ibn Fudayl told us, from 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', regarding a man and a woman who are polytheists and accept Islam. He said: "Their marriage is confirmed. But if one of them accepts Islam before the other, what is between them is severed." He means by that the Magians and polytheists other than the People of the Book.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِي الْمَجُوسِيَّيْنِ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَ أَحَدُهُمَا قَبْلَ صَاحِبِهِ فُرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Manṣūr, from Al-Ḥakam, regarding two Majūs (Zoroastrians): “If one of them accepts Islam before the other, they are separated.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ قَالَ: ثَلَاثٌ لَا يُلْعَبُ بِهِنَّ النِّكَاحُ، وَالْعَتَاقُ، وَالطَّلَاقُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan → Abū al-Dardā’
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, from Abū al-Dardā’, who said: “Three things are not to be played with: marriage, manumission, and divorce.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ سُحَيْمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ قَالَ: أَرْبَعٌ جَائِزَةٌ فِي كُلِّ حَالٍ الْعِتْقُ، وَالطَّلَاقُ، وَالنِّكَاحُ، وَالنَّذْرُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Ḥajjāj → Sulaymān ibn Suḥaym → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib → ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Sulaymān ibn Suḥaym, from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib, from ‘Umar, who said: “Four things are binding in every situation: manumission, divorce, marriage, and vows.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي كِبْرَانَ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: ثَلَاثٌ لَا يُلْعَبُ بِهِنَّ الطَّلَاقُ، وَالنِّكَاحُ، وَالنَّذْرُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Abū Kibrān → Al-Ḍaḥḥāk
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Abū Kibrān, from Al-Ḍaḥḥāk, who said: I heard him say: “Three things are not to be played with: divorce, marriage, and vows.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُهَاجِرٍ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ مَرْوَانَ، وَسُلَيْمَانُ، وَعُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، وَيَزِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ: مَهْمَا أَقَلْتُمُ السُّفَهَاءَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ فَلَا تُقِيلُوهُمُ الطَّلَاقَ، وَالْعَتَاقَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Ayyāsh → ‘Amr ibn Muhājir
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Ayyāsh narrated to us, from ‘Amr ibn Muhājir, who said: ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Marwān, Sulaymān, ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz, and Yazīd ibn ‘Abd al-Malik wrote: “Whatever you pardon the foolish regarding anything, do not pardon them regarding divorce and manumission.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ يُطَلِّقُ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ، يَقُولُ: كُنْتُ لَاعِبًا، وَيُعْتِقُ، ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ يَقُولُ: كُنْتُ لَاعِبًا، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ " {وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا آيَاتِ اللَّهِ هُزُوًا}، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ:" مَنْ طَلَّقَ، أَوْ حَرَّرَ، أَوْ أَنْكَحَ، أَوْ نَكَحَ فَقَالَ: إِنِّي كُنْتُ لَاعِبًا فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus → ‘Amr → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from ‘Amr, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: In the time of ignorance, a man would divorce and then take it back, saying: “I was playing,” and would free a slave and then take it back, saying: “I was playing.” Then Allah revealed: {And do not take the verses of Allah in jest} [Al-Baqarah: 231]. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Whoever divorces, frees, marries off, or marries, and then says: ‘I was playing,’ it is binding.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ الْمُحَارِبِيِّ قَالَ: كَتَبَ إِلَيَّ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ مَهْمَا أَقَلْتَ السُّفَهَاءَ عَنْ أَيْمَانِهِمْ، فَلَا تُقِيلَهُمُ الْعَتَاقَ وَالطَّلَاقَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah → ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz → Sulaymān ibn Ḥabīb al-Muḥāribī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz, from Sulaymān ibn Ḥabīb al-Muḥāribī, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz wrote to me: “Whatever you pardon the foolish regarding their oaths, do not pardon them regarding manumission and divorce.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، فِي رَجُلٍ يَقُولُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: بهشتم، قَالَ: تَطْلِيقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Muṭarrif → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Muṭarrif, from Al-Sha‘bī, regarding a man who says to his wife: “Bahishtum” (Persian for “I have let go”). He said: “It is one divorce.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: طَلَاقُ الْعَجَمِيِّ بِلِسَانِهِ جَائِزٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “The divorce of a non-Arab in his language is valid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي شِهَابٍ، عَنْ جَبَلَةَ بْنِ دَعْلَجٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ قَالَ: "إِذَا طَلَّقَ الرَّجُلُ بِالْفَارِسِيَّةِ قَالَ: يَلْزَمُهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Abū Shihāb → Jabalah ibn Da‘laj → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Abū Shihāb, from Jabalah ibn Da‘laj, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “If a man divorces in Persian, he said: It is binding on her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ رَبِيعٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ يَقُولُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: بهشتم قَالَ: يَلْزَمُهُ الطَّلَاقُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Rabī‘, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who says to his wife: “Bahishtum.” He said: “Divorce is binding on him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِامْرَأَتِهِ: بهشتم، بهشتم، بهشتم، قَالَ: قَدْ قَالَهَا بِلِسَانِهِ، ذَهَبَتْ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Ḥuṣayn → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Abū Ḥuṣayn, from Ibrāhīm, regarding a man who says to his wife: “Bahishtum, Bahishtum, Bahishtum.” He said: “He has said it with his tongue; she is gone from him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ قَالَا: "لَا يَحِلُّ الْخُلْعُ حَتَّى يُوجَدَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى بَطْنِهَا، لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: {إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفَاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ}

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān → His father → Abū Qilābah and Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from his father, from Abū Qilābah and Ibn Sīrīn, who both said: “Khul‘ (divorce at the instance of the wife) is not lawful unless a man is found on her belly, because Allah says: {unless they commit a clear immorality} [Al-Nisa: 19].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: لَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ فِدْيَةً مِنِ امْرَأَتِهِ أَنْ لَا تُطِيعَهُ، وَلَا تَبَرَّ لَهُ قَسَمًا، فَإِنْ فَعَلَتْ ذَلِكَ فَكَانَ مِنْ قِبَلِهَا شَيْءٌ حَلَّتْ لَهُ الْفِدْيَةُ، فَإِنْ أَبَى أَنْ يَقْبَلَ مِنْهَا الْفِدْيَةَ، وَأَبَتْ أَنْ تُطِيعَهُ بَعَثَا حَكَمَيْنِ حَكَمًا مِنْ أَهْلِهِ، وَحَكَمًا مِنْ أَهْلِهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Mughīrah → His father
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from his father, who said: “It is not lawful for him to take a ransom from his wife unless she disobeys him or does not fulfill an oath for him. If she does that and something comes from her side, the ransom is lawful for him. If he refuses to accept the ransom from her and she refuses to obey him, they send two arbiters, one from his family and one from her family.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: إِذَا كَرِهَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ زَوْجَهَا فَلْيَأْخُذْ مِنْهَا وَلْيَدَعْهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “If a woman hates her husband, let him take (ransom) from her and leave her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ مَرْوَانَ الْأَصْفَرِ، عَنْ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحِمْيَرِيِّ، قَالَ: "يَطِيبُ لَكَ الْخُلْعُ إِذَا قَالَتْ: لَا أَغْتَسِلُ لَكَ مِنَ الْجَنَابَةِ، وَلَا أَبَرُّ لَكَ قَسَمًا، وَلَا أُطِيعُ لَكَ أَمْرًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → Marwān al-Aṣfar → Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Ḥimyarī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Marwān al-Aṣfar, from Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Ḥimyarī, who said: “Khul‘ becomes lawful for you if she says: ‘I will not wash myself from Janaabah for you, I will not fulfill an oath for you, and I will not obey a command for you.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: "يَطِيبُ لِلرَّجُلِ الْخُلْعُ إِذَا قَالَتْ: لَا أَغْتَسِلُ مِنَ الْجَنَابَةِ، وَلَا أُطِيعُ لَكَ أَمْرًا، وَلَا أَبَرُّ لَكَ قَسَمًا، وَلَا أَكْرُمُ نَفْسًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Isrā’īl → Jābir → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Yaḥyā → ‘Alī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Isrā’īl, from Jābir, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Yaḥyā, from ‘Alī, who said: “Khul‘ becomes lawful for a man if she says: ‘I will not wash myself from Janaabah, I will not obey a command for you, I will not fulfill an oath for you, and I will not honor myself.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ بَذِيمَةَ، عَنْ مِقْسَمٍ قَالَ: إِذَا عَصَتْكَ أَوْ آذَتْكَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Alī ibn Badhīmah → Miqsam
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from ‘Alī ibn Badhīmah, from Miqsam, who said: “If she disobeys you or harms you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي قَوْلِهِ: {لَا جُنَاحَ}، قَالَ: "ذَلِكَ فِي الْخُلْعِ إِذَا قَالَتْ: لَا أَغْتَسِلُ لَكَ مِنَ الْجَنَابَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding His saying: {there is no blame} [Al-Baqarah: 229], he said: “That is in Khul‘ if she says: ‘I will not wash myself from Janaabah for you.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ السِّجِسْتَانِيِّ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {لِتَذْهَبُوا بِبَعْضِ مَا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفَاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ} قَالَ: إِذَا فَعَلَتْ ذَلِكَ حَلَّ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مِنْهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Muṭarrif → Khālid al-Sijistānī → Al-Ḍaḥḥāk
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Muṭarrif, from Khālid al-Sijistānī, from Al-Ḍaḥḥāk, regarding His saying: {that you may take [back] part of what you have given them unless they commit a clear immorality} [Al-Nisa: 19]. He said: “If she does that, it is lawful for him to take from her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ أَبِي عُتْبَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَخْلَعُ الْمَرْأَةَ قَالَ: إِذَا أَتَى ذَلِكَ مِنْ قِبَلِهَا فَلَا بَأْسَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Abī ‘Utbah → ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abī ‘Utbah narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Malik, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding a man who performs Khul‘ with a woman. He said: “If that comes from her side, there is no harm.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ قَالَ: قَالَ جَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ: إِذَا كَانَ النُّشُوزُ مِنْ قِبَلِهَا حَلَّ لَهُ فِدَاؤُهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Amr ibn Dīnār → Jābir ibn Zayd
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Amr ibn Dīnār, who said: Jābir ibn Zayd said: “If the disobedience is from her side, her ransom is lawful for him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، كَانَ يَقُولُ: "لَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ الْفِدَاءُ، حَتَّى يَكُونَ الْفَسَادُ مِنْ قِبَلِهَا، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ يَقُولُ: لَا تَحِلُّ لَهُ حَتَّى تَقُولَ: لَا أَبَرُّ لَكَ قَسَمًا، وَلَا أَغْتَسِلُ مِنْ جَنَابَةٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Jurayj → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, who used to say: “Ransom is not lawful for him until the corruption is from her side.” He did not say: “It is not lawful for him until she says: ‘I will not fulfill an oath for you, and I will not wash myself from Janaabah.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ طَاوُسٌ يَقُولُ: "يَحِلُّ لَهُ الْفِدَاءُ، بِمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ: {إِلَّا أَنْ يَخَافَا أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ اللَّهِ}، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ يَقُولُ قَوْلَ السُّفَهَاءِ حَتَّى تَقُولَ: لَا أَغْتَسِلُ لَكَ مِنْ جَنَابَةٍ، وَلَكِنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: {إِلَّا أَنْ يَخَافَا أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ اللَّهِ} فِيمَا افْتَرَضَ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا، عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ فِي الْعِشْرَةِ وَالصُّحْبَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Jurayj → Ṭāwūs
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Ṭāwūs used to say: “Ransom is lawful for him based on what Allah said: {unless they fear that they will not keep [within] the limits of Allah} [Al-Baqarah: 229]. He did not say the foolish statement ‘until she says: I will not wash myself from Janaabah for you.’ But he used to say: {unless they fear that they will not keep [within] the limits of Allah} regarding what is obligatory for each of them towards the other in companionship and association.”
